low_level_requirements.requirement: The Quality Checking Services shall determine the observation location associated with each incoming environmental observation prior to applying quality checking rules.

The EMS shall restrict access to environmental metadata according to the restrictions defined in the applicable data sharing agreement.

The QEDC shall retain, for each qualified environmental data record, an association with the originating organization's data sharing agreement.
The QEDS shall restrict dissemination of qualified environmental data to service providers according to the restrictions defined in the applicable data sharing agreement.
The CAS shall provide the current data sharing agreement restrictions to other Clarus services upon request for the purpose of enforcing data and metadata management restrictions.

Clarus is an initiative sponsored by the Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) to organize and make more effective environmental and road condition observation capabilities in support of four primary motivations.
1)	Provide a North American resource to collect, quality control, and make available surface transportation weather and road condition observations so that State Departments of Transportation (DOTs) can be more productive in maintaining safety and mobility on all roads.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall provide an interface through which weather service providers can submit weather data for receipt by the system.
The Clarus system shall be able to receive weather data submissions from multiple weather service providers concurrently.
The Clarus system shall verify that received weather data conforms to the required data format before accepting it.
The Clarus system shall generate an acknowledgment to the weather service provider upon successful receipt of weather data.
The Clarus system shall log the identity of the weather service provider and timestamp for each received weather data submission.
The Clarus system shall reject and report weather data submissions that do not conform to the required data format.

The Schedule Service shall automatically resume previously scheduled tasks upon restart following an unexpected shutdown.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Schedule Service shall accept and maintain schedule definitions that specify when data dissemination requests are to be executed.
The Schedule Service shall trigger a data dissemination request at the time specified by the associated schedule.
The Qualified Environmental Data Services shall receive the triggered dissemination request from the Schedule Service.
The Qualified Environmental Data Services shall retrieve the qualified environmental data corresponding to the parameters of the scheduled request.
The Qualified Environmental Data Services shall disseminate the retrieved data to the recipient(s) identified in the scheduled request.
The Configuration & Administration Service shall store and provide the schedule configuration data used by the Schedule Service to generate scheduled dissemination requests.
The Configuration & Administration User Interface shall allow an authorized user to define, modify, and remove scheduled data dissemination requests.

The Clarus Initiative consists of two development components. 
•	The first component is the development of the Clarus system – a network for sharing, quality controlling, and exchanging surface environmental data and relevant surface transportation conditions. 
•	The second component is the development of tools (such as decision support systems) that make effective use of the Clarus system. 
The addition of Clarus system data to national weather observations will further enhance general purpose weather forecasting, providing a wider range of benefits to the protection of life an

[Context 5 | source=clarus_high.txt | chunk=6 | similarity=0.591695]
em owners and their contractors;
•	Surface transportation weather service providers (which may include the observation system owners);
•	The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA);
•	General weather service providers;
•	Research community; and
•	Climate data warehouse and other non-surface weather interests.
The deployed Clarus system is envisioned to include:
•	A one-stop Internet portal for all surface transportation environmental observations;
